bailey_yi.name: Bailey Yi
bailey_yi.summary:
Representing Indiana, Bailey Yi is in the early chapters of a competitive journey, currently competing at the U13 level, with 4 entries across 2 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

Bailey is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

bala_sabarish_venkatakrishnan.name: Bala Sabarish Venkatakrishnan
bala_sabarish_venkatakrishnan.summary:
Out of Michigan, Bala Sabarish Venkatakrishnan has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U13 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 8 entries in 3 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

Notably, Bala has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Bala is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Bala has built chemistry with several doubles partners:
  • Rajguru Reddy Are — 3 events in BD, best: #3

Bala has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

bavish_dornala.name: Bavish Dornala
bavish_dornala.summary:
Representing Illinois, Bavish Dornala has stepped onto the competitive stage with 2 tournament entries. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Bavish's.

Bavish competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

bavish_reddi_dornala.name: Bavish Reddi Dornala
bavish_reddi_dornala.summary:
A Illinois competitor, Bavish Reddi Dornala has collected 2 tournament titles, currently competing at the U17 level, capturing 2 titles across 14 tournaments and 35 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Bavish keeps finding ways to come out on top.

Notably, Bavish has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Bavish competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

Bavish has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 7 different players:
  • Nithesh Madishetti — a trusted BD partner (7 events, best: #4)
  • Sunny Zheng — 4 events, 1 title together in BD

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 2 titles across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Bavish is still ascending.

Bavish has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Bavish isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 2 titles, Bavish is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

bella_hu.name: Bella Hu []
bella_hu.summary: Bella Hu [] is just getting started on the junior circuit with 1 tournament entry. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Bella's.

bella_liu.name: Bella Liu
bella_liu.summary:
Out of California, Bella Liu has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U13 GD, U13 XD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 15 career titles across 25 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Bella is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (5 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Bella is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 3 singles titles, 6 doubles titles, 4 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U13 GD, U13 XD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (GD U13) • 5 ORC titles.

Bella has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 18 different players:
  • Katherine F Xu — 9 events in GD, producing 2 titles together
  • Sophia Liu — 8 events in GD/U13, producing 4 titles together
  • Enming Cao — 6 events, 1 title together in XD

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 5 podium finishes across 4 tournaments. Bella is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Bella has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Bella has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 9 seeded entries across the career, Bella consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 15 titles, Bella has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

Hailing from Georgia, Bivab Chandra has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-2), currently competing at the U15 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Bivab's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

A true triple-threat, Bivab competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (2 entries) • ORC top-2 finisher.

With 22 different doubles partners across the career, Bivab adapts to anyone:
  • Yashas Cheerlavancha — 5 events, 1 title together in BD
  • Taneesh Musunuru — a trusted BD partner (4 events, best: #2)
  • Roger Kung — 2 events in BD, best: #4

A veteran of the junior circuit, Bivab has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Bivab has competed across 4 different regions (Midwest, NE, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title, Bivab is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

bliss_liu.name: Bliss Liu
bliss_liu.summary:
Out of California, Bliss Liu broke through with a tournament title, currently competing at the U15 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 43 entries across 17 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

A true triple-threat, Bliss competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, a doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (2 entries).

Bliss has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 17 different players:
  • Elisha Gian Chang — a trusted BD partner (4 events, best: #5)
  • Yian Yang — 2 events in BD, best: #2

Bliss has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning NE, NorCal, SoCal, Bliss isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

Over 5 seasons and 1 title, Bliss has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

boqian_zheng.name: Boqian Zheng
boqian_zheng.summary:
Representing California, Boqian Zheng wears the gold from Junior Nationals — the single most important tournament for any American junior player in BD U11. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 3 career victories across 18 tournaments, Boqian has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Notably, Boqian has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Boqian competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #3, 3 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals champion (BD U11) • 2 ORC titles.

With 15 different doubles partners across the career, Boqian adapts to anyone:
  • Aadya Yanamandra — a trusted XD partner (7 events, best: #3)
  • Magadheesan Velu — 6 events in BD, producing 3 titles together
  • Aiden Wu — a trusted BD partner (4 events, best: #3)

Boqian has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Boqian has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 10 seeded entries across the career, Boqian consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Boqian brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

brendan_chen.name: Brendan Chen
brendan_chen.summary:
Representing Illinois, Brendan Chen has conquered the Open Regional Championship stage, capturing an impressive 3 ORC titles across 3 regional championships. ORCs are the highest level of regular-season competition, drawing the strongest fields in each region. Winning at this level repeatedly marks Brendan as one of the premier players on the circuit.

Notably, Brendan has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Brendan especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 6 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (5 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (9 entries) • 3 ORC titles.

The statistics paint a vivid picture — Brendan boasts 22 three-game battles — proving Brendan is no stranger to the pressure of a decider, a solid 69% win rate over 86 matches, 49 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively.

Brendan has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 22 different players:
  • Ishi Reddy — 6 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Clarinda Jin — a trusted Mixed/U17/XD partner (6 events, best: #3)
  • Allen w Yang — a trusted BD partner (5 events, best: #3)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 7 podium finishes across 5 tournaments. Brendan is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Brendan has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Brendan has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 20 seeded entries across the career, Brendan consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 4 seasons and 7 titles, Brendan has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

brianna_lee.name: Brianna Lee
brianna_lee.summary:
A California competitor, Brianna Lee has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U15 GS, U15 GD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 7 career titles across 17 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Brianna is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

What makes Brianna especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 3 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U15 GS, U15 GD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (GD U15) • 4 ORC titles.

With 14 different doubles partners across the career, Brianna adapts to anyone:
  • Micah Cruz — 7 events in GD/U15, producing 2 titles together
  • Max Zhang — a trusted U15/XD partner (5 events, best: #4)

Brianna has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Brianna has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Brianna has been seeded #1 or #2 in 6 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

Over 4 seasons and 7 titles, Brianna has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

bryan_liu_zhou.name: Bryan Liu Zhou
bryan_liu_zhou.summary:
Out of Washington, Bryan Liu Zhou is writing the opening pages of a badminton story, currently competing at the U17 level, with 3 entries across 1 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

A true triple-threat, Bryan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

bryan_xie.name: Bryan Xie
bryan_xie.summary:
Hailing from New York, Bryan Xie is in the early chapters of a competitive journey, currently competing at the U17 level, with 4 entries across 2 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

Bryan competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

bryan_zhang.summary: Representing New York, Bryan Zhang has stepped onto the competitive stage with 1 tournament entry. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Bryan's.

bryant_leonardo_hu.summary:

Bryant is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

bryce_xie.name: Bryce Xie
bryce_xie.summary:
Representing New York, Bryce Xie has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-2), currently competing at the U13 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Bryce's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Bryce has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Bryce especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 3 doubles titles, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries) • ORC top-2 finisher.

With 9 different doubles partners across the career, Bryce adapts to anyone:
  • Zechariah Wang — 14 events in BD, producing 2 titles together
  • Vaishnavi Packiyaraj — 10 events in XD, producing 2 titles together

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 1 title across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Bryce is still ascending.

Bryce has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Bryce has competed across 4 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 10 seeded entries across the career, Bryce consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 5 titles, Bryce is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.
